notes: Data sourced from official university websites. Fees verified as of research date.

universities.name: TUM – Technical University of Munich

bachelor.notes: Usually €2,000 or €3,000/semester depending on the specific program. Exact amount listed on each program's details page. Fees introduced from WS2024/25.

master.notes: Usually €4,000 or €6,000/semester depending on the specific program. Some programs (e.g., Bioinformatics, Quantum Science & Technology, Software Engineering) are tuition-free even for non-EU students — check individual program pages. Students who completed a prior bachelor's at TUM or another German university may be exempt.

phd.notes: Doctoral candidates are fully exempt from tuition fees.

universities.waivers: Merit/need-based scholarship grants of €500–1,800/semester available. Up to ~20% of students may receive waivers. Exemptions also for: students with established domestic connections (5+ years residence in Germany, or one parent employed in Germany 3+ years), students with disabilities, asylum seekers.
universities.additional_fees: Semester fee (~€97–150) applies to all students regardless of tuition, varying by campus location.

universities.name: Heidelberg University

bachelor.notes: €1,500/semester for all bachelor's and consecutive master's programs in Baden-Württemberg. Introduced WS2017/18.

master.notes: €1,500/semester applies to consecutive master's programs. Same rate as bachelor's.

phd.notes: PhD/doctoral candidates are explicitly exempt from the non-EU tuition fee in Baden-Württemberg.

universities.waivers: Exemptions for: EU/EEA citizens, students with German higher education entrance qualification (Abitur), exchange students (Erasmus etc.), refugees, non-EU citizens with 5+ years legal employment in Germany, those who completed prior degree in Germany.

universities.name: KIT – Karlsruhe Institute of Technology

master.notes: €1,500/semester — same rate applies to consecutive master's programs.

phd.notes: Doctoral candidates exempt from non-EU tuition fee (Baden-Württemberg policy).

universities.waivers: Same BW-wide exemptions apply (EU/EEA, Abitur holders, exchange students, refugees, prior German degree).

phd.notes: Doctoral candidates exempt from non-EU tuition fee (Baden-Württemberg policy).
Doctoral candidates exempt from non-EU tuition fee (Baden-Württemberg policy).
Doctoral candidates exempt from non-EU tuition fee (Baden-Württemberg policy).
Doctoral candidates exempt from non-EU tuition fee (Baden-Württemberg policy).

universities.name: Frankfurt School of Finance & Management

bachelor.notes: €8,200/semester. The BSc program runs 7 semesters (3.5 years); total tuition ~€57,400. Programs: BSc Business Administration, BSc Computational Business Analytics.

master.notes: Varies by program. Master in Management (MSc, 4 semesters): €35,500 total (~€8,875/semester). Master of Finance (MSc, 4 semesters): €42,000 total (~€10,500/semester). Other MSc programs also ~€35,500. Per-year cost approximately €17,750–21,000.

mba.notes: Full-Time MBA total tuition €42,000 (plus €400 enrollment fee). Early-bird discounts up to €6,000 off. Program is ~1 year.

universities.waivers: Merit-based scholarships: 15%, 25%, 50%, 75%, or 100% tuition reduction. President's Scholarship covers 75% tuition + free accommodation in year 1. Klaus-Peter Müller Scholarship covers full tuition for select Master/MBA students.

master.notes: Total tuition €36,000 for all MSc programs (Global Management, Analytics & AI, Innovation & Entrepreneurship). Paid in 3 installments over the 2-year program. Covers all courses, seminars, teaching materials, orientation, and career support.

full_time_mba.notes: Full-Time MBA total tuition €50,000. Includes books, study materials, and Smart Certificate. Does not include travel for study trips/exchanges.

executive_mba.notes: Executive MBA total tuition €64,800. Includes teaching fees, required cases and books, e-learning resources, meals on campus, and Smart Certificate. Paid in 3 equal installments over 3 years.

universities.name: WHU – Otto Beisheim School of Management

bachelor.notes: €9,100/semester for BSc in International Business Administration. Program is 6 semesters; total tuition ~€54,600.

master.notes: Master in Management (MSc): €33,000 total for 90 ECTS track (includes Capstone Module abroad); €40,400 for 120 ECTS track (includes semester abroad). Payment schedule: €2,000 deposit + 3 semester installments. ~40% of MSc students receive partial scholarships.

mba.notes: Full-Time MBA total tuition €49,500. Includes all tuition, study materials (print & digital), accommodation/meals during international modules, German language instruction, and semester transport ticket in NRW. International travel not included. Early-bird discounts: up to €6,000 off.

universities.waivers: ~40% of MSc students receive WHU scholarships covering at least 25% of tuition. MBA partial scholarships available for highly talented candidates from all countries.

universities.name: Constructor University Bremen (formerly Jacobs University)

bachelor.notes: €10,000/semester (€20,000/year). 3-year BSc program; total tuition ~€60,000. Additional university/admin fees ~€696/semester. Fact sheet for 2025–26 confirms €20,000/year tuition.

master.notes: €10,000/semester (€20,000/year). Additional university fee ~€120 + semester ticket ~€480. Merit-based scholarships up to €10,000 available. Tuition Deferral Program available (pay after graduation).

phd.notes: PhD students are NOT charged tuition fees. PhDs are typically fully funded positions. Confirmed on official financing pages.

universities.waivers: Merit-based scholarships (up to €10,000) for bachelor and master students. Tuition Deferral Program allows deferral until after graduation and employment.
